title: Cognitive Behavior Therapy -- Clinical Significance
content: Cognitive behavior therapy is a structured, didactic, and goal-oriented form of therapy. The approach is hands-on and practical wherein the therapist and patient work in a collaborative manner with the goal of modifying patterns of thinking and behavior to bring about a beneficial change in the patient's mood and way of living his/her life. It is used to help a wide range of problems, and appropriate treatment protocols are applied depending on the diagnosis and problems the patient is facing. [8] [9] [10] [11] Most psychotherapists who practice CBT personalize and customize the therapy to the specific needs of each patient.
